subject: Chemistry
solution:
Properties of the diamagnetic materials are: The diamagnetic materials have all the paired electrons, and none of the electrons is the valence, resulting in the absence of atomic dipoles in these materials. This happens because the overall magnetic moment of each atom in the compound cancels out.

NiCO4→ diamagnetic, sp3 hybridization, number of unpaired electrons =0

If the substance contains unpaired electrons, it is a paramagnetic substance.

NiCl42−, → paramagnetic, sp3 hybridization, number of unpaired electrons =2

subject: Chemistry
options: A, B, C, D and E
solution:
Statement A is correct. The oxalate ion ($C_2O_4^{2-}$) is a bidentate ligand and forms chelate rings with the central metal ion. Due to the chelate effect, $[Fe(C_2O_4)_3]^{3-}$ is more stable than complexes formed by monodentate ligands like $[Fe(OH)_6]^{3-}$ and $[Fe(SCN)_6]^{3-}$.

Statement B is incorrect. Ethylenediamine (en) is a bidentate ligand and forms chelate rings, whereas $NH_3$ is a monodentate ligand. Therefore, $[Cu(en)_2]^{2+}$ is more stable than $[Cu(NH_3)_4]^{2+}$ due to the chelate effect.

Statement C is correct. In $K_4[Fe(CN)_6]$, the oxidation state of Fe is $+2$, which corresponds to a $3d^6$ configuration. Since $CN^-$ is a strong field ligand, it causes the pairing of electrons in the $3d$ orbitals, leaving two inner $3d$ orbitals empty. Thus, the hybridization is $d^2sp^3$.

Statement D is correct. The $NO_2^-$ ligand is an ambidentate ligand, meaning it can coordinate to the metal center through either the nitrogen atom or the oxygen atom. Therefore, $[Fe(NO_2)_3Cl_3]^{3-}$ can exhibit linkage isomerism.

Statement E is incorrect. Both $NO_2^-$ and $SCN^-$ are ambidentate ligands. $NO_2^-$ can bind through N or O, and $SCN^-$ can bind through S or N.

Therefore, the correct statements are A, C, and D.

Answer: A, C and D only

subject: Chemistry
solution: Alcohol upon reaction with protic acids tends to lose a molecule of water to form alkenes. These reactions are known as dehydrogenation or dehydration of alcohols. It is an example of an elimination reaction.The order of the ease of dehydration of alcohols is: tertiary > secondary > primary.

subject: Chemistry
solution: In the most common bridging mode, denoted μ2 or simply μ, the CO  ligand bridges a pair of metals. This bonding mode is observed in the commonly available metal carbonyls: Co2CO8, Fe2CO9, Fe3CO12and Co4CO12. In certain higher nuclearity clusters, CO bridges between three or even four metals.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
This octahedral complex is of M(AA)2b2 type structure in which AA is bidentate ligand and b is monodentate ligand. This can exist in two geometrical isomeric form cis and trans. Out of which trans form is relatively more stable isomer. Now due to ambidentate ligand SCN- it can also shows linkage isomersim.

Cuen2SCN2

So, relatively more stable trans isomers will have three possible isomers.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
In TiH2O63+, oxidation state of Titanium is +3.
Thus, electronic configuration:

Ti+3:3d1⇒t2g1,0,0,eg0,0

We know CFSE =-0.4nt2g+0.6negΔo       (      Δo = Octahedral splitting energy)

Δo=96×1030.4×6×1023=4×10-19 J

Using planck's equation and fact that octahedral splitting energy per complex is:
⇒Δo=hcλ=4×10-19 J

⇒λ=6.4×10-34×3×1084×10-19=4.8×10-7 m

⇒λ=480×10-9 m

∴λ=480 nm

subject: Chemistry
solution:
From the below image it is clear that two isomers are produced if the reactant complexion is a cis-isomer, as well as only one isomer, is produced if the reactant complex is a trans-isomer.

Two isomers are produced if the reactant complex ion is a cis-isomer because the cis isomer can further form facial and meridional isomers.

Only one isomer is produced if the reactant complex ion is a trans-isomer because it cannot form any isomeric isomers.

Trans → only meridonial

Cis → fac and mer

solution:
The ligand carbon monoxide has no charge, hence metals have zero oxidation state in both Ni(CO)4 and Fe(CO)5. Low oxidation states are found when a complex has ligands capable of π-acceptor character in addition to the σ-bonding.

In the complexes, NiCO4 & FeCO5, the ligand CO is π-acceptor character in addition to the σ-bonding.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The electronic configuration of Co2+ is 3d7. In an octahedral complex of Co2+ with H2O ligands, the d orbitals split into two sets of energy levels: t2g (lower energy) and eg (higher energy).

In this case, since the complex is octahedral, we have six ligands surrounding the central cobalt ion. Each ligand donates a pair of electrons to the cobalt ion, occupying the available d orbitals.

So, CoH2O62+=d7High spin=t2g5 eg2

In the t2g set of orbitals, there will be 1 unpaired electron

                         = 3 B.M .

Ligands which have two different donor sites but at a time connects with only one donor site to central metal are ambidentate ligands.
Ambidentate ligands are $\mathrm{NO}_2^{-} ; \mathrm{SCN}^{-} ; \mathrm{CN}^{-}$

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Wacker process:

The process of oxidation of terminal alkenes to aldehydes and ketones by using Palladium and Copper compounds as catalysts is called the Wacker process.

Palladium (II) chloride and Copper (II) chloride are used in this process along with Oxygen and water.

CH2=CH2 →O2,H2OPdCl2,CuCl2CH3CHO

The catalyst used in Wacker's process is PdCl2

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Around the manganese atom, there are five CO ligands and one Mn occupying octahedron vertices. For octahedral geometry, the more appropriate hybridization is d2sp3 that produces six hybrid orbitals pointing towards CO and Mn.

The octahedral shape of molecules is the shape of molecules where six atoms or ligands or groups of atoms are arranged in a systematic way around a central dogma or atom. The Octahedral Shape of Molecules molecules has eight faces.

solution:
Metal ion having either d5 or d10 configuration can be filled symmetrically in both t2g and eg orbitals. Co+2 has d7 configuration, Co+3 has d6 configuration. Mn+2 has d5 configuration but CN- is strong field ligand and it will pair the electrons due to which electronic configuration is t2g5eg0.

As F is weak field ligand hence back pairing of electrons do not take place or in case of weak field ligand pairing energy of electrons (P )is greater than crystal field splitting energy in octahedral complex ∆o i.e.P>∆o , hence electrons will go in eg orbitals, instead of back paired in t2g orbitals.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The crystal field splitting energy $\Delta_0$ depends on the strength of the ligand.

According to the spectrochemical series, the order of ligand strength is $F^- < H_2O < en < CN^-$.

Thus, the order of $\Delta_0$ for the given complexes is $[CrF_6]^{3-} < [Cr(H_2O)_6]^{3+} < [Cr(en)_3]^{3+} < [Cr(CN)_6]^{3-}$.

Matching with the given values:
$[CrF_6]^{3-}$ has the minimum $\Delta_0 = 15,060$ cm$^{-1}$ (B $\rightarrow$ I).
$[Cr(H_2O)_6]^{3+}$ has $\Delta_0 = 17,400$ cm$^{-1}$ (C $\rightarrow$ II).
$[Cr(en)_3]^{3+}$ has $\Delta_0 = 22,300$ cm$^{-1}$ (D $\rightarrow$ III).
$[Cr(CN)_6]^{3-}$ has the maximum $\Delta_0 = 26,600$ cm$^{-1}$ (A $\rightarrow$ IV).

This corresponds to A-IV, B-I, C-II, D-III.

Answer: A-IV, B-I, C-II, D-III

subject: Chemistry
solution:
According to spectrochemical series, ligands can be arranged in a series in the order of increasing field strength as given below: 

I –  < Br–  < SCN –  < Cl –  < S2–  < F –  < OH –  < C2O4 2–  < H2O < NCS – < edta4–  < NH3  < en < CN –  < CO

As per the above series, correct answer is S2-<C2O42-<NH3<en<CO
